title: SACRAMENTO - With unanimous, bipartisan support, legislation to increase access to critical healthcare services has passed the Assembly’s Aging and Long-Term Care Committee. AB 540
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "PACE provides vulnerable seniors with affordable, high-quality, and comprehensive care.",
    "PACE has saved the state of California tens of millions of dollars annually.",
    "PACE serves low-income, vulnerable seniors by providing all-inclusive Medicare and Medi-Cal services.",
    "PACE achieves positive care outcomes through fewer hospitalizations and nursing home admissions, better health and quality of life, and high rates of participant satisfaction.",
    "PACE is projected to save California $131 million this year because it is up to 42% less expensive than institutional care.",
    "Many seniors enrolled in PACE are diagnosed with dementia, with the majority of dementia cases caused by Alzheimer's Disease.",
    "PACE helps people live with dignity by enabling them to strengthen their relationships through long-term home and community-based care.",
    "The PACE program is a proven model and a successful alternative to nursing home care.",
    "72% of the eligible population in California does not know about the existence of PACE.",
    "Many seniors with higher needs could benefit from direct state engagement and referrals for PACE services."
  ]
}
```
